diagrams.title: Two-Level Open File Table
diagrams.description: Shows the relationship between per-process file descriptor table and system-wide open file table
elements: File Descriptor 3 → Points to System Entry
File Descriptor 4 → Points to System Entry
System-Wide Open File Table Entry 1: offset=150, mode=RDWR, inode pointer → /home/user/file.txt
System-Wide Open File Table Entry 2: offset=500, mode=RDONLY, inode pointer → /var/log/data.log
I-Node Table: contains file metadata and disk block pointers
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how file descriptors relate to system tables and how multiple processes can share the same file with independent positions

diagrams.title: File Operations Flow
diagrams.description: Diagram showing the sequence of operations on a file from creation to closing
diagrams.purpose: Show the lifecycle of file operations and how they depend on each other

diagrams.title: File Position Pointer Movement
diagrams.description: Visual representation of file pointer during various operations on a 500-byte file
elements: After write(50): Position = 150 (file now extends to 650 bytes)
diagrams.purpose: Understand how file position changes with different operations and seek origins
